chunks.text: Course: EE 225D | EECS at UC Berkeley EE 225D. Audio Signal Processing in Humans and Machines Catalog Description: Introduction to relevant signal processing and basics of pattern recognition. Introduction to coding, synthesis, and recognition. Models of speech and music production and perception. Signal processing for speech analysis. Pitch perception and auditory spectral analysis with applications to speech and music. Vocoders and music synthesizers. Statistical speech recognition, including introduction to Hidden Markov Model and Neural Network approaches. Units: 3 Also Offered As: ELENG 225D Prerequisites: EL ENG 123 and STAT 200A; or graduate standing and consent of instructor. Formats: Spring: 3 hours of lecture per week Fall: 3 hours of lecture per week Grading Basis: Student Option Final Exam Status: No Links: EECS Course Archives (CalNet authentication required) CS enrollment policies EE enrollment policies HKN course maps
